responses.text: The provided article addresses the "Momo challenge," an internet phenomenon that allegedly involved a grotesque figure coercing children into dangerous tasks or self-harm via platforms like YouTube and WhatsApp.
The "challenge" is widely recognized as a viral hoax that gained traction due to unverified rumors spread by concerned parents, local news outlets, and law enforcement agencies.
There are no confirmed reports of any child harming themselves or being forced to participate in the challenge.
The unsettling image used to depict "Momo" is actually a cropped photograph of a 2016 sculpture named "Mother Bird," created by a Japanese artist for the special effects company Link Factory.
Tech companies, including YouTube, have officially stated that they found no evidence of videos promoting the Momo challenge on their platforms.
The author concludes that adults sharing these unsubstantiated rumors actually legitimize the hoax, thereby failing to model the very online literacy and skepticism they want to teach children.
